task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless execution handoff preview that wires the pre-execution boundary into preview execution jobs and work orders. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add sessions, passwords, JWT validation, external auth, credential storage, provider calls, quota or billing mutation, worker execution, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, or API keys.
Execution handoff preview must deny live/provider/worker/result-write paths until hosted auth, vault-handle, quota-reservation, worker-lease, and idempotent-commit gates exist.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this handoff preview composes existing work-order and pre-execution boundary vocabulary instead of enabling execution.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing execution queue, work-order, pre-execution boundary, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse the existing Pro queue work-order payloads, domain preflight payload, API route handlers, web fetchJson helper, and preview-only boundary panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic handoff preview payload that combines a queued work order with the pre-execution boundary and reports live execution denied until hosted prerequisites are satisfied.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
Credential values are never accepted, displayed, stored, or returned by this handoff preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue tests prove the handoff preview denies execution and contains both work-order and pre-execution blockers.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed handoff preview payload and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the handoff panel, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real provider execution, credential access, quota mutation, or auth enforcement.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless execution handoff preview endpoint per preview job.
Pro web shell renders whether a queued work order can hand off to future execution and which hosted prerequisites block it.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local handoff preview that composes the existing denied work order and pre-execution boundary. It prepares an explicit execution handoff payload without enabling provider calls, credential reads, quota reservations, worker execution, or result writes.
